questions.question: What is the umbra of a shadow?
questions.answer: The umbra is the darkest part of a shadow where light is completely blocked.

questions.question: What is the penumbra of a shadow?
questions.answer: The penumbra is the lighter part of a shadow where light is partially blocked.

questions.question: Which type of mirror diverges light rays?
questions.answer: A convex mirror diverges light rays.

questions.question: Which type of mirror converges light rays?
questions.answer: A concave mirror converges light rays.

questions.question: What is the law of reflection?
questions.answer: The angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection.

questions.question: What type of image does a plane mirror form?
questions.answer: A plane mirror forms a virtual, upright, and laterally inverted image.

questions.question: Why are shadows longer in the morning and evening?
questions.answer: Because the Sun is low in the sky, causing light rays to fall at a shallow angle.

questions.question: Why do convex mirrors provide a wider field of view?
questions.answer: Because they diverge light rays and reduce image size.

questions.question: What is lateral inversion?
questions.answer: Lateral inversion is the left-right reversal of an image in a plane mirror.

questions.question: Why does a concave mirror form a magnified image for a nearby object?
questions.answer: Because the object is within the focal length, causing diverging rays that appear larger.

questions.question: What is diffused reflection?
questions.answer: Reflection of light from rough surfaces in many directions, forming no clear image.

questions.question: What is regular reflection?
questions.answer: Reflection of light from smooth surfaces in a single direction, forming clear images.

questions.question: What happens to shadows when the light source is moved closer?
questions.answer: Shadows become larger as light rays diverge more.

questions.question: What happens to shadows when the light source is moved farther?
questions.answer: Shadows become smaller as light rays are more parallel.

questions.question: Why are images in a concave mirror inverted when the object is beyond the focal point?
questions.answer: Because the reflected rays converge to form a real image on the opposite side of the mirror.

questions.question: What type of image does a convex mirror always form?
questions.answer: A virtual, upright, and diminished image.

questions.question: Why do we need diffused reflection to read printed text?
questions.answer: It scatters light evenly from the paper surface so the eyes can read it clearly.

questions.question: What is the effect of a point light source on shadows?
questions.answer: It produces sharp and well-defined shadows.

questions.question: What is the effect of an extended light source on shadows?
questions.answer: It produces blurred or fuzzy shadows due to overlapping rays.

questions.question: Why does a concave mirror form a real image that can be projected on a screen?
questions.answer: Because the reflected rays converge at a point in front of the mirror forming a real image.

questions.question: How does a sundial use shadows to tell time?
questions.answer: The moving shadow of the gnomon indicates time on hour markings.

questions.question: How do periscopes use mirrors?
questions.answer: Plane mirrors reflect light down the periscope tube into the viewer’s eyes to see over obstacles.

questions.question: What is the focal point of a concave mirror?
questions.answer: The point where parallel rays of light converge after reflection.

questions.question: Why are convex mirrors used in vehicle rear-view mirrors?
questions.answer: They provide a wider field of view and diminished images for safety.

questions.question: Why do plane mirrors not change the size of the image?
questions.answer: Because the image distance equals the object distance and the reflected rays remain parallel.
